qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Remote panic via send_window integer overflow in crafted WindowUpdate frames
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding identifies the same vulnerable code (FlowController::increase_send_window_by checked_add overflow) and root cause (WindowUpdate credit overflow) as the advisory.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unsafe deserialization in import endpoint permits attacker-controlled code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding describes a different vulnerability (deserialization RCE) unrelated to the advisory's Yamux WindowUpdate overflow.

selected_finding.title: Remote panic via send_window integer overflow in crafted WindowUpdate frames

target_advisory_judgement.reason: Finding matches advisory-described vulnerable code (FlowController::increase_send_window_by overflow) and root cause (crafted WindowUpdate credit). Existing poc_report.json confirms the vulnerability was reproduced, but no fresh PoC execution was performed under .judge/poc_runs for this judgement.
evidence: Finding vulnerability_report.json identifies increase_send_window_by checked_add overflow at flow_control.rs:179
Finding describes same attack scenario: crafted WindowUpdate with large credit causing u32 overflow and panic
poc_report.json confirms reproduction with panic caught via std::panic::catch_unwind
blockers_to_higher_tier: No fresh PoC execution under .judge/poc_runs to satisfy exploit_success_predicate or bug_activation_predicate
